bugs.title: ListChatSessions messageCount always 0
bugs.description: ListChatSessions returns messageCount=0 for a session that has 14 messages persisted (verified via GetChatHistory). The message count field is not being updated when messages are added.
bugs.expected: messageCount should reflect actual number of messages in the session (14)
bugs.api_command: curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8090/toqui.v1.ChatService/ListChatSessions -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Origin: http://localhost:3000' -H 'Authorization: Bearer TOKEN' -d '{"trip_id": "TRIP_ID"}'

bugs.title: Usage count off by one
bugs.description: Sent 7 messages total (1 selection + 4 planning + 2 companion) but /api/usage reports used=6. One message appears to not have been counted, possibly the selection mode message that triggered trip creation.

ux_issues.description: The AI created 29 itinerary items in a single tool call for days 1-7, then had to make a second tool call for days 8-10. This resulted in a long pause mid-stream while the first batch was processed. Breaking into smaller batches (e.g., 2-3 days at a time) would feel more responsive.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider chunking large itinerary creation into smaller batches to improve perceived responsiveness.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: No expert persona handoff (suggest_expert) was triggered despite asking specifically about wildlife-watching for kids, which could have matched a nature/family expert.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Asked about sloths, frogs, wildlife sanctuaries, and guided tours. The AI gave a comprehensive answer without handing off to an expert persona. This is acceptable behavior but a missed opportunity for showcasing the persona system.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: Excellent family trip planning experience. The AI demonstrated strong context retention -- after trip creation with Costa Rica as the destination, it never asked where we were going. The 10-day itinerary was well-paced with realistic drive times (3h SJO to Arenal, 5-6h Arenal to Manuel Antonio). Safety responses were outstanding: specific hospital names, activity-by-activity water safety assessment for a non-swimming 6-year-old, and appropriate caution about night exploration. The Frog Pond Night Tour for Noah and sloth-watching recommendations for Emma showed excellent personalization. Companion mode responses were appropriately concise compared to planning mode. The only weakness was no expert persona handoff despite wildlife-focused questions.
